---
title: "B | Thema 2"
format: live-html
engine: knitr  
status: "Version 0.1"
lang: de 
---

{{< include _extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}
 
## Reliabilität -- McDonald's ω vs. Cronbach's α

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: false

webr::install("quadprog",  repos = "https://kroehne.github.io/webr-quadprog-repo")
 
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(mnormt))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(lavaan))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(lavaanPlot))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(psych))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(semTools))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(DT))

assignInNamespace("lav_options_checkinterval", function(x, nm, num2int) {
    if (any(is.na(x))) return(TRUE)
    if (num2int) x <- as.integer(x)                                                                                              
    oks <- vapply(x, function(x1) {
      (x1 > nm$bounds[1] || (x1 == nm$bounds[1] && nm$first.in)) &&                                                              
      (x1 < nm$bounds[2] || (x1 == nm$bounds[2] && nm$last.in))                                                                  
    }, TRUE)
    all(oks)                                                                                                                     
  }, ns = "lavaan")

model_3f <-  "visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3
              textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6
              speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9"

fit_3f <- cfa(model_3f, data = HolzingerSwineford1939)


View <- function(dat){
  dat_name <- deparse(substitute(dat))
  df_display <- dat |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "❌ (NA)", .x))
  )
  
  DT::datatable(
    df_display,
    rownames = FALSE, 
    options = list(
      pageLength = 5,
      scrollX = TRUE,
      dom = 'tip',   
      columnDefs = list(list(className = 'dt-center', targets = "_all"))
    ),
    caption = htmltools::tags$caption(
      style = 'caption-side: top; text-align: left;',
      'data.frame: ', htmltools::strong(dat_name)
    )
  )  
}
```

### Präsentation

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: true
# =============================================================
# Thema 02: Reliabilität: McDonald's ω, Cronbach's α und 
#           Composite Reliability
# Datensatz: HolzingerSwineford1939 (lavaan) + psych::bfi
# =============================================================

library(lavaan)
library(psych)
library(semTools)

# ---- PRÄSENTATION: α vs. ω für eine Skala ----

data(HolzingerSwineford1939)
dat_hs <- HolzingerSwineford1939

# CFA für den Textual-Faktor (x4, x5, x6)
model_text <- 'textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6'
fit_text <- cfa(model_text, data = dat_hs)
summary(fit_text, standardized = TRUE)

# Cronbach's Alpha
alpha_result <- psych::alpha(dat_hs[, c("x4", "x5", "x6")])
cat("Cronbach's α:", round(alpha_result$total$raw_alpha, 3), "\n")

# McDonald's Omega (aus CFA-Ladungen)
ss <- standardizedSolution(fit_text)
lambdas <- ss$est.std[ss$op == "=~"]
thetas <- ss$est.std[ss$op == "~~" & ss$lhs %in% c("x4", "x5", "x6") &
                       ss$lhs == ss$rhs]
omega <- sum(lambdas)^2 / (sum(lambdas)^2 + sum(thetas))
cat("McDonald's ω (manuell):", round(omega, 3), "\n")

# Composite Reliability via semTools
cr <- compRelSEM(fit_text)
cat("Composite Reliability (semTools):", round(cr, 3), "\n")

# Wann weichen α und ω ab?
cat("\nStandardisierte Ladungen:", round(lambdas, 3), "\n")
cat("Sind die Ladungen gleich (tau-äquivalent)?",
    ifelse(max(lambdas) - min(lambdas) < 0.1, "≈ ja", "nein"), "\n")
cat("→ Bei tau-Äquivalenz: α ≈ ω. Sonst: α unterschätzt die Reliabilität.\n")
```

## Bericht

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: true
# ---- BERICHT: α und ω für alle drei HS-Faktoren ----

models_hs <- list(
  Visual  = 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3',
  Textual = 'textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6',
  Speed   = 'speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'
)

results <- data.frame(Faktor = character(), alpha = numeric(), omega = numeric(),
                      CR = numeric(), max_min_lambda = numeric(),
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

for (fac in names(models_hs)) {
  items <- switch(fac,
    Visual  = c("x1", "x2", "x3"),
    Textual = c("x4", "x5", "x6"),
    Speed   = c("x7", "x8", "x9"))

  # Alpha
  a <- psych::alpha(dat_hs[, items])$total$raw_alpha

  # CFA
  fit <- cfa(models_hs[[fac]], data = dat_hs)
  ss <- standardizedSolution(fit)
  lam <- ss$est.std[ss$op == "=~"]
  the <- ss$est.std[ss$op == "~~" & ss$lhs %in% items & ss$lhs == ss$rhs]

  # Omega
  om <- sum(lam)^2 / (sum(lam)^2 + sum(the))

  # Composite Reliability
  cr_val <- compRelSEM(fit)

  results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
    Faktor = fac, alpha = round(a, 3), omega = round(om, 3),
    CR = round(cr_val, 3), max_min_lambda = round(max(lam) - min(lam), 3)))
}

print(results)
cat("\n→ Je größer max_min_lambda, desto stärker die Verletzung der\n",
    "  tau-Äquivalenz und desto mehr unterschätzt α die Reliabilität.\n")

# Formel-Herleitung:
# ω = (Σλ_i)² / [(Σλ_i)² + Σθ_ii]
# α = (p / (p-1)) * (1 - Σσ²_i / σ²_total)
# Bei gleichen Ladungen (τ-Äquivalenz): α = ω

```
